intro: Wigginsia calvescens, also known as the Wigginsia plant, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Linderniaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Wigginsia calvescens include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Wigginsia calvescens is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the form of infusions and extracts to harness its health benefits.
